title: Codebase Audit — Dead Code & Security Gaps
topic: Deep-scan audit: orphaned/unused code + security gaps + orphaned files; severity 1–5 heat-mapped table with fix per row.
summary: Three-scan audit of dead code, security gaps, and orphaned files; heat-mapped 1–5 with concrete fixes.
content:
Contents
  1. Overview
  2. Methodology
  3. Heat-Mapped Findings
  4. Security Gaps — Detail
  5. Dead Code — Detail
  6. Orphaned Files & Config — Detail
  7. Top Fixes (Ranked)
  8. Story Risk Review (Code-Base Impact)
  9. How to Read the Heat Map

Overview

This paper is the end-of-PLA-0006-week deep audit of the Vector codebase, conducted on 2026-05-04. Three parallel investigator agents ran independent scans across the full repository — one targeting security gaps, one hunting dead code and unused dependencies, and one cataloguing orphaned files and configuration holes. The scope spanned backend/, app/, dev/, root config files, and .claude/worktrees/.

The audit produced approximately 110 findings in total: 17 at Severity 5 (exploitable now / delete immediately / sensitive doc unprotected), 22 at Severity 4 (exploitable with conditions / very likely dead), 19 at Severity 3 (defense-in-depth gaps), 24 at Severity 2 (hardening / nice-to-have), and 9 at Severity 1 (informational). The single highest-priority finding in the whole paper is an untracked, un-gitignored sensitive document at Sec Audit/security_sensitive_data_20260425.md — one stray git add . would commit it to the repository. Every other fix flows from there.

Methodology

Three parallel Opus investigator agents ran for approximately five minutes each. The security agent grepped all middleware, handler, and config files for authentication, rate-limiting, CORS, CSP, audit-logging, and token-handling patterns. The dead-code agent cross-referenced TypeScript exports against all import sites using symbol-count heuristics (file count, not line count) to avoid false positives, and matched Go exported identifiers against call-sites. The orphaned-files agent compared git status --porcelain output against .gitignore coverage and inspected directory sizes and branch lists.

A Sonnet writer agent synthesised the three scan outputs into this paper. Items confirmed already-fixed were excluded: the login open-redirect at app/login/page.tsx:14-19, the /api/dev/research path-traversal cap, the CSP connect-src ws:// allowance, and the addressables-register CSRF fix. Conventions confirmed safe and not flagged: all fmt.Sprintf SQL sites use whitelist enums (SQL injection sweep clean); app/api/dev/library/file/route.ts path-resolve is safe via startsWith check plus .md suffix guard.

Heat-Mapped Findings

Rows ordered by severity descending; within the same severity, grouped by area (Security → Dead Code → Orphaned Files → Config Hygiene).

# Area Finding Location Severity Fix
1 Security /api/env/switch unauthenticated shell-exec endpoint backend/cmd/server/main.go:353-397 5 Add RequireAuth + RequirePermission; fix inverted env check that currently allows staging.
2 Security JWT accepted via ?access_token= querystring on every route backend/internal/auth/middleware.go:39 5 Gate querystring fallback behind r.URL.Path == "/ws" only.
3 Security clientIP() blindly trusts X-Forwarded-For backend/internal/auth/handler.go:277-289, backend/internal/users/handler.go:214-226 5 Configure trusted-proxy whitelist via chi/middleware.RealIP; align rate-limit IP with audit IP.
4 Dead Code 8 dead .tsx components with zero importers (AppShell, AppContent, AppHeader, BlockingReleaseGate, DraftBanner, PillToggle, Navigation, d_store_app_name-index) app/components/AppShell.tsx et al. 5 Delete after confirming no dynamic-import string-match consumers.
5 Dead Code 6 unused npm icon packages declared in package.json package.json 5 Run npm uninstall @heroicons/react d3-selection feather-icons iconoir lucide-react react-feather.
6 Orphaned Files Sensitive audit doc untracked + un-gitignored — one git add . commits it Sec Audit/security_sensitive_data_20260425.md 5 Add Sec Audit/ to .gitignore immediately; git rm --cached if already staged; consider moving to local-assets/.
7 Config Hygiene .gitignore coverage holes for 7 sensitive/artifact directories .gitignore 5 Add audits/, Sec Audit/, test-results/, dev/reports/, .agents/, .codex/, .playwright-mcp/ to .gitignore.
8 Security WebSocket InsecureSkipVerify: true — CORS does not validate WS upgrade backend/internal/realtime/client.go:61 4 Set InsecureSkipVerify: false; configure OriginPatterns from FRONTEND_ORIGIN env.
9 Security No rate limit on /api/auth/refresh, /api/auth/logout, /api/auth/change-password backend/cmd/server/main.go:402,403,409 4 Add httprate.LimitByIP to each auth sub-route.
10 Security No rate limit on /api/admin/* and /api/roles/* groups backend/cmd/server/main.go:792-866 4 Add r.Use(httprate.LimitByIP(120, time.Minute)) at group level.
11 Security Refresh-cookie clearRefreshCookie lacks dual-path clear backend/internal/auth/handler.go:266-275 4 Issue a second clear cookie with Path: "/" alongside the existing path-scoped clear.
12 Security /healthz and /api/status/pipeline unauthenticated, leaking db_host and BACKEND_ENV backend/cmd/server/main.go:299-341 4 Authenticate /api/status/pipeline; redact db_host from /healthz response.
13 Security CORS AllowedOrigins permits empty string when FRONTEND_ORIGIN unset backend/cmd/server/main.go:267 4 Make FRONTEND_ORIGIN required for all envs; panic on startup if unset.
14 Dead Code LayerHierarchyDiagram.tsx — zero importers app/(user)/portfolio-model/LayerHierarchyDiagram.tsx 4 Delete after grepping for dynamic-import string references.
15 Dead Code GraphCanvas.tsx — zero importers app/lib/graph-engine/view/GraphCanvas.tsx 4 Delete or wire up to a consuming route.
16 Dead Code 4 unused Go functions: ValidateName, ValidateKind (addressables) + 2 Parse* siblings backend/internal/addressables/service.go:877,885 4 Delete or wire to validation pipeline; run go vet ./... to confirm zero callers.
17 Dead Code 4 unused skeleton variants: CardSkeleton, TableRowSkeleton, NavItemSkeleton, PortfolioModelSkeleton app/components/SkeletonCompositions.tsx:13,51,65, app/components/SvgSkeleton.tsx:111 4 Delete all four exported variants; confirm zero consumers first.
18 Dead Code 2 likely-unused npm packages: d3-zoom (verify vs DiagramCanvas), selenium-webdriver + types (Playwright is runner) package.json 4 Confirm d3-zoom usage in DiagramCanvas; uninstall selenium-webdriver and @types/selenium-webdriver.
19 Dead Code dev/store/ui_apps/ui_app_name/ scaffold orphan directory (3 files) dev/store/ui_apps/ui_app_name/ 4 Delete; only app/store/registry.ts references the correctly-prefixed copy.
20 Orphaned Files 17 .claude/worktrees/ totalling 2.6 GB; 4 are prunable zombies (checkout lives in PM repo) .claude/worktrees/ 4 Run git worktree prune then delete the 4 prunable zombies; review remaining 13.
21 Security No audit event for CSRF mismatch backend/internal/security/csrf.go:67-73 3 Emit audit.Log("security.csrf_mismatch", ...) on every rejected CSRF check.
22 Security No audit event on RequirePermission denials backend/internal/auth/middleware.go:80-129 3 Audit-log auth.permission_denied with user, permission code, and path on every rejection.
23 Security No audit event on RequireAuth failures (expired/missing JWT) backend/internal/auth/middleware.go:30-63 3 Audit-log expired and missing JWT events with path and remote IP.
24 Security dev/cgl-volatile-do-not-commit/ committed despite name; contains raw innerHTML concat dev/cgl-volatile-do-not-commit/.../fakechat/server.ts:279 3 Add directory to .gitignore; run git rm --cached; replace innerHTML concat with safe DOM construction.
25 Security Hardcoded absolute path for env-switch script backend/cmd/server/main.go:373 3 Read from BACKEND_SWITCH_SCRIPT env var or compute relative to binary location.
26 Security /api/auth/me not rate-limited backend/cmd/server/main.go:412-416 3 Add httprate.LimitByIP(120, time.Minute) to the /me route.
27 Security Permissions[] array exposed in plaintext on /login and /me responses backend/internal/auth/handler.go:67,102-108 3 Send only the boolean flags the UI actually needs; strip full permission code array from response.
28 Security Rate limits per-IP only — not per-user on authenticated routes backend/cmd/server/main.go:404,605 3 Compose IP+UserID key for authenticated routes; IP+target-email for /password-reset.
29 Security Refresh-token reuse logs but does not revoke the session backend/internal/auth/service.go:183 3 On reuse detection, run UPDATE sessions SET revoked = TRUE WHERE user_id = $1 to invalidate all sessions.
30 Security Header.tsx SDK help fallback may bypass server sanitisation app/components/Header.tsx:97-99,193 3 Run __html through DOMPurify on the client side before assignment.
31 Security DevPlansPanel.tsx renders plan fields as raw HTML without sanitisation dev/pages/DevPlansPanel.tsx:113,118,143 3 Sanitise via DOMPurify or convert scope/value/features_extended fields to plain text.
32 Security Ranking PermissionChecker for work_item returns true, nil unconditionally backend/cmd/server/main.go:238-240 3 Wire permResolver.Has(ctx, userID, permissions.WorkItemsReorder) as the real check.
33 Security audit.Logger.Log swallows DB write errors silently backend/internal/audit/audit.go:34-38 3 Emit bootstatus.Set("audit_log", false, err) and log.Printf("AUDIT-DROP") on error.
34 Security marked.parse() un-sanitised in LibraryPage.tsx dev/pages/LibraryPage.tsx:76 3 Configure marked with sanitiser option or apply DOMPurify post-pass on output.
35 Dead Code IsPAdmin exported function — zero callers backend/internal/artefacts/handler.go:338 3 Delete function; confirm no external package imports it.
36 Dead Code ParseEntityKey exported function — zero callers backend/internal/nav/bookmarks.go:82 3 Delete or wire to a real call site; add story reference if deferred.
37 Orphaned Files api-reference/ 362 MB tree — not confirmed in .gitignore api-reference/ 3 Confirm tracked status; add to .gitignore or move to submodule if vendored.
38 Orphaned Files test-results/.last-run.json untracked Playwright artifact test-results/.last-run.json 3 Delete and add test-results/ to .gitignore.
39 Orphaned Files 15 stranded local claude/<adjective-name-hash> branches + 7 stale feature branches Local git branches 3 Confirm with user; delete after worktree prune via git branch -d for each.
40 Security CSRF cookie has no Max-Age/Expires — lives indefinitely in browser backend/internal/security/csrf.go:27-37 2 Set MaxAge: 86400 (24 h) on the CSRF cookie.
41 Security Login error reveals "account locked" vs "invalid credentials" vs "inactive" distinction backend/internal/auth/handler.go:122-134 2 Return single 401 "invalid credentials" to caller; log specific reason internally only.
42 Security audit_log rows lack method, path, and user-agent fields backend/internal/audit/audit.go:19-27 2 Extend Entry struct with Method, Path, UserAgent; populate from request context.
43 Security JWT_ACCESS_SECRET empty case proceeds silently without error backend/internal/auth/tokens.go:71-79 2 Add explicit early-return error when secret is empty; panic on startup in non-dev envs.
44 Security Email format not validated before DB lookup backend/internal/auth/handler.go:120,211 2 Call mail.ParseAddress; return 400 on malformed email before any DB query.
45 Security locale query param not whitelisted — accepts arbitrary string backend/internal/addressables/handler.go:358 2 Validate against regex ^[a-z]{2}(-[A-Z]{2})?$; reject 400 on mismatch.
46 Security No per-user WebSocket connection limit backend/internal/realtime/client.go 2 Cap clients[userID] map at 5 concurrent connections per user.
47 Security unsafe-inline + unsafe-eval in CSP script-src next.config.ts:9,11 2 Move toward nonces; remove unsafe-inline from script-src incrementally.
48 Dead Code ldap_stub.go 17-line placeholder with no consumer backend/internal/auth/ldap_stub.go 2 Delete or annotate with a // PLA-XXXX: story reference.
49 Dead Code 2 graph-engine interaction TODOs with no implementation body app/lib/graph-engine/interactions/hover.ts:28, app/lib/graph-engine/interactions/drag.ts:37 2 Implement or remove with a story reference pointing to the backlog card.
50 Orphaned Files Migration sequence gap: 027 missing between 026 and 028 db/schema/ 2 Investigate the gap; document deletion rationale or renumber to fill the sequence.
51 Orphaned Files Migration duplicate prefix 097: both a forward and an untracked DOWN file use same number db/schema/097_page_help_rich_content.sql, 097_page_help_rich_content_DOWN.sql 2 Renumber DOWN file to 098 or move to db/rollbacks/ directory.
52 Config Hygiene dev/plans/ registry gap: PLA-0002 slot missing dev/plans/ 2 Reconcile plan registry; document the gap in docs/c_plan_index.md.
53 Config Hygiene 3 stale planning markdowns (~170 KB total, Apr 25) superseded by current schema docs db-architecture-audit-comparison.md, -sonnet.md, -v7-migration.md 2 Move to dev/planning/archive/ to declutter the root.
54 Config Hygiene 25 root-level .png screenshots tracked in git (~2 MB) Repository root (*.png) 2 Move to /public/screenshots/ or add *.png root glob to .gitignore.
55 Security No CSP report-uri / report-to — policy violations are invisible next.config.ts 1 Add report-to directive pointing to a logging endpoint for visibility.
56 Security processStartedAt exposed unauthenticated on /healthz (minor fingerprinting) backend/cmd/server/main.go:299-341 1 Omit processStartedAt from the unauthenticated health response.
57 Security Confirmed-safe dangerouslySetInnerHTML sources (Header.tsx help body, R*.json content) — server-sanitised app/components/Header.tsx, research panel 1 No action needed; document the sanitisation chain in a code comment for future auditors.
58 Dead Code ranking.Register over-engineered for single registrant — work_item only backend/cmd/server/main.go:235 1 Informational only; registry shape is correct; keep as-is.
59 Config Hygiene .mcp.json.disabled referenced in c_playwright.md but file does not exist (doc drift) .claude/commands/c_playwright.md 1 Either create the disabled file or remove the reference from the doc.
60 Config Hygiene Empty dev/launcher/ directory (0 B, created Apr 21) dev/launcher/ 1 Delete the empty directory.
61 Config Hygiene local-assets/backups/ 18 MB / 30+ files / 7+ days oldest local-assets/backups/ 1 Run <backupsql> retention pass to prune old backups.

Security Gaps — Detail

This section covers Severity 5 and 4 security findings only. Severity 3 and below are covered in the heat map table.

S5-1 — /api/env/switch: Unauthenticated Shell Execution

An attacker who can reach the backend network (or exploit any SSRF) can invoke /api/env/switch with a crafted POST body and cause the server to execute a shell script with no authentication check. The existing CSRF token provides only moderate friction — it is not a substitute for RequireAuth. Worse, the env-guard logic is currently inverted, meaning a staging env call is permitted when it should be blocked. This is a pre-auth remote code execution primitive on any deployment where the backend is not fully private.

S5-2 — JWT via Querystring on Every Route

The middleware at backend/internal/auth/middleware.go:39 accepts a JWT in the ?access_token= querystring parameter on all routes, not just the WebSocket handshake. This means tokens can appear in server logs, CDN access logs, Referer headers, and browser history on any route a user visits — a permanent credential-leak vector. The querystring fallback must be restricted to /ws only.

S5-3 — Blind X-Forwarded-For Trust

clientIP() uses the first value of X-Forwarded-For without validating that it came from a trusted proxy. Any request with a spoofed header can bypass IP-based rate limits and make rate-limit audit logs appear to originate from arbitrary addresses. The fix is to use chi/middleware.RealIP with an explicit trusted-proxy CIDR list.

S4-1 — WebSocket InsecureSkipVerify: true

With TLS verification disabled on the WebSocket client, a network-level attacker (or a compromised internal service) can perform a man-in-the-middle attack on real-time traffic. Additionally, the WS upgrade is not validated against Origin, so a cross-site WebSocket hijack (CSWSH) is feasible from any page that can trigger an upgrade. Setting InsecureSkipVerify: false and restricting OriginPatterns to FRONTEND_ORIGIN closes both vectors.

S4-2 — No Rate Limit on Core Auth Routes

/api/auth/refresh, /api/auth/logout, and /api/auth/change-password have no rate limiting. The refresh endpoint is particularly valuable to an attacker — unlimited refresh attempts allow persistent token-farming without triggering any throttle. Admin and roles API groups have the same gap, enabling enumeration and brute-force of admin actions at full speed.

S4-3 — Refresh Cookie Partial Clear

clearRefreshCookie issues a clear directive scoped to a specific path but does not issue a second clear for Path: "/". If the refresh cookie was ever set or accessible at the root path (e.g., via a past config change), it survives logout and can be replayed. Issuing a dual-path clear is a trivially safe fix.

S4-4 — Unauthenticated Status Endpoints Leak Infra Data

/healthz returns db_host and /api/status/pipeline returns BACKEND_ENV — both without any authentication. These fields give an attacker a free enumeration of the infrastructure topology, database host naming, and current environment. The pipeline endpoint should require auth; the healthz endpoint should redact db_host.

S4-5 — CORS Permits Empty AllowedOrigins

When FRONTEND_ORIGIN is not set (common on fresh dev or staging spins), the CORS middleware inserts an empty string into AllowedOrigins. Depending on the CORS library behaviour, this may match all origins or produce unpredictable results. Making FRONTEND_ORIGIN a required startup check eliminates the ambiguity.

Dead Code — Detail

Replaced Shell-Layer Cluster

Four components — AppShell.tsx, AppContent.tsx, AppHeader.tsx, and Navigation.tsx — all have zero importers and appear to be remnants of an aborted or completed layout migration. The current shell is delivered by a different component tree. These files add noise to the component directory, risk confusion when a developer searches for "navigation" or "app shell", and bloat the TypeScript compilation unit. They should be deleted after a quick dynamic-import string search confirms no lazy-loaded references.

Six-Icon-Library Bloat

package.json declares six icon libraries: @heroicons/react, d3-selection, feather-icons, iconoir, lucide-react, and react-feather. A symbol-import grep across app/ and dev/ finds zero consumers — only react-icons is actually imported. These six packages inflate node_modules, slow installs, and may cause bundler over-inclusion if tree-shaking is not perfectly configured. All six can be uninstalled with a single npm uninstall command.

Skeleton Family Unused

Four skeleton loading-state variants are exported from SkeletonCompositions.tsx and SvgSkeleton.tsx — CardSkeleton, TableRowSkeleton, NavItemSkeleton, and PortfolioModelSkeleton — with zero consumers anywhere in the codebase. These likely existed for a loading-state pass that was never completed or was replaced with a different pattern. Deleting them removes dead surface area from the component API.

Orphaned Files & Config — Detail

CRITICAL: Sec Audit/security_sensitive_data_20260425.md

This is the single highest-priority fix in the entire paper. The file contains sensitive security audit findings and is sitting in the working tree untracked and un-gitignored. A single git add . or git add -A — a command any developer runs dozens of times a day — will commit it to the repository history permanently. Once pushed, the data is effectively public to all repository clones and cannot be fully erased without a history rewrite.

Immediate actions (in order): (1) Add Sec Audit/ to .gitignore right now. (2) Confirm it is not already staged with git status. (3) If staged, run git rm --cached "Sec Audit/security_sensitive_data_20260425.md". (4) Consider moving the file to local-assets/ which is already excluded from commits.

S5 — Seven .gitignore Coverage Holes

Beyond the Sec Audit directory, six other artifact and tooling directories are missing from .gitignore: audits/, test-results/, dev/reports/, .agents/, .codex/, and .playwright-mcp/. These directories appear in git status as untracked, meaning they are one bulk-add away from being committed. Adding all seven to .gitignore in a single commit eliminates the risk.

S4 — 2.6 GB Worktree Accumulation

The .claude/worktrees/ directory holds 17 worktrees totalling 2.6 GB. At least 4 are prunable zombies — their Git branch checkouts reference the PM repository, not this one, so git worktree list shows them as broken. Running git worktree prune will remove the stale entries; the 4 broken directories can then be deleted from disk. The remaining 13 should be reviewed — any older than 14 days with no recent commits should be pruned.

Top Fixes (Ranked)

Ranked by the product of severity, ease of fix, and blast-radius (impact if left unfixed). All items below can be resolved in under 30 minutes each.

Rank Fix File Effort
1 Add 7 directories to .gitignore (including Sec Audit/) .gitignore 2 min
2 Restrict JWT querystring to /ws path only backend/internal/auth/middleware.go:39 5 min
3 Add RequireAuth + RequirePermission to /api/env/switch backend/cmd/server/main.go:353 10 min
4 Configure trusted-proxy whitelist for clientIP() backend/internal/auth/handler.go:277 10 min
5 Make FRONTEND_ORIGIN required at startup; panic if unset backend/cmd/server/main.go:267 5 min
6 Uninstall 6 dead icon packages + selenium-webdriver package.json 3 min
7 Redact db_host from /healthz; authenticate /api/status/pipeline backend/cmd/server/main.go:299 10 min
8 Fix WebSocket InsecureSkipVerify + add OriginPatterns backend/internal/realtime/client.go:61 15 min
9 Add rate limits to auth refresh, logout, change-password routes backend/cmd/server/main.go:402-409 10 min
10 Delete 8 dead .tsx shell-layer components app/components/AppShell.tsx et al. 10 min
11 Wire WorkItemsReorder permission — remove unconditional true return backend/cmd/server/main.go:238 10 min
12 Run git worktree prune and delete 4 prunable zombie worktrees .claude/worktrees/ 5 min

Story Risk Review (Code-Base Impact)

Each of the 27 proposed fixes was scored a second time, this time for code-base impact risk — i.e., how badly a botched implementation could break the running system. This is independent of the security severity (1–5) above. Severity answers "how dangerous is the gap?"; risk answers "how dangerous is the fix?".

Rubric

  • HIGH — touches middleware, auth, or session paths that run on every request; a regression has wide blast-radius.
  • MED — narrower contract change (single endpoint, audit write, package config) where mistakes are recoverable but user-visible.
  • LOW — file deletes, gitignore tweaks, docs, or housekeeping that the compiler / lockfile / type-checker will catch.

Distribution: 5 HIGH · 11 MED · 11 LOW.

Sequencing recommendation

Work the LOW-risk wave first to clear noise and confirm tooling, then MED, then the HIGH-risk middleware/auth changes once the team has rebuilt confidence in CI signal.

  1. Wave 1 — LOW (10 stories): #4, #5, #7, #8, #12, #15, #16, #17, #19, #24, #26 — gitignore, deletes, package prune, docs.
  2. Wave 2 — MED (12 stories): #2, #6, #10, #11, #13, #14, #18, #20, #21, #25, #27 — endpoint contracts, audit writes, migrations, rate limits.
  3. Wave 3 — HIGH (5 stories): #1, #3, #9, #22, #23 — middleware, auth, session, WS gating. Land each behind a feature flag where possible; require integration-test sign-off.

How to Read the Heat Map

The Severity column uses a 5-to-1 scale rendered red-to-green. A 5 (red) means the finding is exploitable now without preconditions, a file should be clearly deleted, or a sensitive document is one command away from public exposure — fix before the next commit. A 4 (orange) means exploitable under realistic conditions (requires adjacent access, specific timing, or minor precondition) or the code is very likely dead with high confidence — fix this sprint. A 3 (amber) means a defense-in-depth gap: nothing is broken today but the absence of the control makes a future breach harder to detect or contain — schedule for next sprint. A 2 (lime) means a hardening or hygiene improvement — low urgency, high value if batched. A 1 (green) is informational: noted for awareness, no immediate action required.
